source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Loss of Vessel Station Keeping

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Eugene Island Block 355A

actions_taken: Well secured by closing downhole safety valve
Temporary flange cap installed on tubing head
New production tree installed on 6 October 2014
Diagnostic checks completed by FMS, USCG, and third-party contractors
Sea trials conducted to recreate incident circumstances
Incident reported to USCG National Response Center
event.summary: On 1 October 2014 at approximately 17:00 hours, the offshore supply vessel (OSV) Endurance, a dynamically-positioning (DP) capable vessel, lost station keeping while conducting well operations on Well A002 at Eugene Island Block 355A platform. The vessel moved off location, causing the Well A002 production tree to be severed at the wellhead flange and fall overboard. A lubricator connected to the wireline unit fell onto the platform deck, damaging handrails and grating. An estimated 300 ft by 50 ft area of oil was spilled. Investigation revealed that a recently installed communications tower located within 1 foot of the DP system caused electrical interference, resulting in simultaneous failure of the DP system, gyro, and throttles. No injuries were reported.
recommendations: Add a question in e-Well to identify the type of work vessel including DP vessels and capture the vessel's DP capabilities
Develop an interim SOP to evaluate DP vessels for various offshore well operations until improved regulatory guidelines are established by the USCG and/or BSEE
Implement policy that no communications equipment can be located within 1 meter (3.28 ft) of the throttle control computer, DP computer or gyros
Utilize safety break-away hose coupling in future applications

hazards.name: Loss of dynamic positioning system capability
Rigid high-pressure piping connection to offshore platform

threats.name: DP system failure due to electrical interference
Shearing of production tree due to vessel movement

consequences.name: Structural damage to platform handrails and grating
Loss of containment - oil spill to offshore waters

controls.name: Dynamic positioning system
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel station keeping to prevent movement away from platform
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to maintain safe vessel position during well operations

supporting_text: the FMS Endurance lost its station keeping and started moving away from the EI-355A platform
the DP system was failing to maintain the vessel's position
the DP system, gyro and throttles all went out at once due to some type of electrical interference

controls.name: Bow thruster alarm system
controls.barrier_role: Alert crew to DP system anomalies
controls.lod_basis: Detection system to alert crew of DP system issues before loss of station keeping

supporting_text: the bow thruster alarm had sounded several times on 1 October 2014, but each time it was able to reset into the DP mode without losing station keeping
at 16:34 hours, the bow thruster alarm sounded again and although power was not loss, the DP system was failing to maintain the vessel's position

controls.name: Manual vessel control (throttles)
controls.barrier_role: Allow manual intervention to regain vessel control when DP system fails
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to manually manage vessel position when automated systems fail

supporting_text: the captain took over manual controls and put both throttles full astern, but the vessel continued to move forward due to its initial momentum

controls.name: Downhole safety valve
controls.barrier_role: Isolate well and prevent uncontrolled flow after production tree severance
controls.lod_basis: Passive safety device to prevent loss of containment after structural failure

supporting_text: The well was secured by closing the downhole safety valve

controls.name: Emergency disconnect coupling (absent)
controls.barrier_role: Allow quick disconnect of rigid piping in event of vessel loss of station keeping
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical safety device to prevent production tree severance during vessel movement

supporting_text: The possible contributing cause can be attributed to the absence of an emergency disconnect coupling within the rigid hard line piping from the FMS Endurance and Well A002 that would allow a quick disconnect of the piping in the event of a loss of station keeping by the vessel

controls.name: Communications equipment placement standards
controls.barrier_role: Prevent electrical interference with critical DP system components
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent hazardous equipment placement near vital systems

supporting_text: The FMS Endurance had a communication tower recently installed and according to the communication tower vendor, it should not have been placed near any vital GPS equipment
the communications tower was located 6 inches (in) from the throttle system boards, 28 in from the gyro system and the DP system was approximately 1 ft below the communication tower

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
